mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in FEDFUNDS decline periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a labor market cooling that historically precedes downward earnings revisions, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real GDP growth and consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 change × RSAFS change) shows no predictive power for 20d SPX returns (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, aggressive Fed tightening compresses equity multiples via higher discount rates and stagflation fears, yielding negative forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the interaction IC drops below 0.02 over a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in INDPRO expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1.4 million units signals weakening construction demand and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and depresses returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline following HOUST drops below 1.4 million, or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between strong employment growth and rising unemployment indicates a structural labor mismatch that increases wage-push inflation, forcing tighter financial conditions and negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The cross-product of PAYEMS month-over-month change and UNRATE level shows no statistically significant relationship with 20d SPX returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, consumer credit expansion amplifies spending momentum, driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not elevated when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% alongside RSAFS expansion, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.
